description: TeamStation static, offline-only registry of publicly disclosed TeamViewer client CVEs that affect operator launch decisions. Advisory only — TeamStation never phones home and never auto-updates the installed TeamViewer client.
source: Curated by TeamStation maintainers from public TeamViewer security bulletins and MITRE CVE entries. See docs/teamviewer-reference.md for the maintainer update procedure.

entries.title: TeamViewer Full Client / Host pre-authentication bypass on incoming session prompts
entries.summary: An incoming-session confirmation step in TeamViewer Full Client and Host could be bypassed under specific configurations, allowing a remote attacker who already knows a peer ID to start a session without the prompted user confirmation. Mitigated in 15.74.5 by hardening the confirmation gate so the dialog is required regardless of supplied command-line credentials.
entries.remediation: Update the installed TeamViewer Full Client / Host to 15.74.5 or later.

entries.title: TeamViewer URI handler argument injection via NTLM relay primitive
entries.summary: TeamViewer Desktop did not quote arguments passed to its custom URI scheme handlers, allowing a crafted teamviewer10:// (and sibling tv*1://) URI to inject additional command-line parameters and trigger SMB authentication to attacker-controlled hosts. Mitigated in 15.8.3 by quoting argv. TeamStation additionally blocks --play / --control / --Sendto / UNC shapes inside the validator regardless of the installed client.
entries.remediation: Update the installed TeamViewer Desktop / Host to 15.8.3 or later. TeamStation already blocks the known argv-injection shapes at the launcher boundary.
